## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** HashiCorp Consul — a service-mesh / service-discovery control plane. Go backend + an Ember UI (`ui/packages`, ignore for this ticket). Index: 3,297 files, 26,653 symbols; heaviest areas `agent/consul` (5,191 symbols), `agent/structs`, `agent`.

**Layout that matters here:**

| Path | Role |
|---|---|
| `agent/agent.go:234` | `Agent` — the process-level object; runs HTTP/DNS/gRPC and holds a `delegate` (`agent/agent.go:249`) that is *either* `*consul.Server` or `*consul.Client` |
| `agent/consul/server.go:169` | **`consul.Server` — the object being reworked.** ~275 lines of struct fields |
| `agent/consul/*_endpoint.go` | net/rpc endpoints, each embedding `*Server` |
| `agent/consul/*_backend.go`, `raft_handle.go`, `tenancy_bridge.go`, `v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go`, `acl_server.go`, `leader_connect_ca.go` | **adapter/wrapper types** — thin structs holding `*Server`, handed to subsystems as narrow interfaces |
| `agent/consul/server_grpc.go` | central gRPC wiring: `registerACLServer`, `registerPeerStreamServer`, … (lines 339–545) |
| `agent/grpc-external/services/*` | external gRPC services, each declaring its *own* local `Backend`/`ACLResolver`/`StateStore`/`FSMServer` interfaces |
| `agent/grpc-internal/services/subscribe` , `agent/rpc/{operator,peering}` | internal gRPC services, same pattern |
| `internal/storage/raft`, `internal/multicluster/...` | v2 resource stack, reaches the server through `Handle` / `AggregatedConfig` |

**The pattern to know:** almost nothing outside `agent/consul` names `*consul.Server`. Subsystems declare a local one-to-four-method interface and retain that. `agent/consul` supplies a satisfier — usually a one-field wrapper (`raftHandle{s *Server}`, `PeeringBackend{srv *Server}`) or `s` itself.

## Step 2 — The server lifecycle contract today

**Construction:** `consul.NewServer(config, flat Deps, externalGRPCServer, incomingRPCLimiter, serverLogger)` — `agent/consul/server.go:467`. Called from `agent.Agent.Start` (`agent/agent.go:600`, assigned at `agent/agent.go:709`). It is a single ~410-line constructor that builds the struct literal (`server.go:502–520`) and then wires every subsystem in place — there is no second `Start()` phase, so *construction is the wiring point*.

**Shutdown:** `(*Server).Shutdown()` — `agent/consul/server.go:1241`. Idempotent via `s.shutdown`; closes `s.shutdownCh` (`:1250`), stops leader routines (`:1254`), serf LAN/WAN, router, raft, listener, internal gRPC handler, conn pool, ACLResolver, then `s.fsm.State().Abandon()` (`:1301`). `(*Server).Leave()` — `agent/consul/server.go:1330` — graceful pre-shutdown. ## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

`sense_blast(consul.Server)`: **risk high**, 176 direct callers, 211 affected symbols, 80 affected files, 109 production / 61 test, 55 affected test files, 31 interface-retained holders.

**Tier 1 — no compile-time tie to `consul.Server`; a lifecycle change reaches them silently.** These are the ones that ship a dead handle.

1. **Closure-captured `s` in `server_grpc.go`** — `aclgrpc.Config` (5 closures, `server_grpc.go:357–370`), `GetStore` in peerstream/connectca/dataplane (`:387`, `:479`, `:499`), all `ForwardRPC` closures. Highest risk: a closure pins the *old* `*Server` with zero type signal anywhere, and `go vet`/compiler will never flag it. If the server is replaced these keep calling the dead one.
2. **`raft.Backend.handle` / `forwardingClient.handle`** — `internal/storage/raft/backend.go:80`, `forwarding.go:115`. Separate module tree, `Handle` interface, `b.Run(ctx)` goroutine started at `agent/consul/server.go:531` bound to `shutdownCh`. A dead handle here silently misroutes v2 resource writes.
3. **`v1compat.reconciler.config`** — `internal/multicluster/internal/controllers/v1compat/controller.go:82`; the shim also runs its own supervisor loop (`agent/consul/v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go:36`) with an `eventCh` that survives independently.
4. **`rate.Handler.serversStatusProvider`** — `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:168`, registered from *two* places (`server.go:525`, `agent/agent.go:708`). A stale provider makes `IsLeader()` lie to every RPC.
5. **`local.State.Delegate`** — `agent/local/state.go:172`; assigned once at `agent/agent.go:762` and never re-assigned. Anti-entropy would talk to a dead server forever.
6. **`agent.Agent.delegate`** — `agent/agent.go:249`; three concrete type assertions (`:1668`, `:1790`, `:1865`) that will compile fine but branch wrongly if the concrete type changes.
7. **`peerstream.subscriptionManager.backend` / `exportedServiceRequest.sub` / `submatview` materializer** — `subscription_manager.go:46`, `subscription_view.go:27,78`. Per-peering, long-lived streams; they outlive the registration call and hold the backend across reconnects.
8. **`ca.ConsulProvider.Delegate`** — `agent/connect/ca/provider_consul.go:35`, re-narrowed a second time at `leader_connect_ca.go:462`. Two indirections from the server; leaf signing goes wrong silently.

**Tier 2 — interface-typed but inside `agent/consul`, so a compile break is likely.** `ACLResolver.backend` (`acl.go:281`), `CAManager.delegate` (`leader_connect_ca.go:61`), `AutoConfig.backend` (`auto_config_endpoint.go:159`), `GatewayLocator.srv` (`gateway_locator.go:46`), `IndexReplicator.Delegate` (`replication.go:247`), `reporting.ReportingManager.server` (`reporting.go:17`).

**Tier 3 — background goroutines pinned to `shutdownCh`.** Every `go …(&lib.StopChannelContext{StopCh: s.shutdownCh})` at `agent/consul/server.go:531, 568, 570, 666, 670, 739, 752, 781, 784, 787, 788, 822, 832, 865, 1033`. If the rework introduces a new server without closing the old `shutdownCh` (`Shutdown` at `:1250` is the *only* closer, and it's `s.shutdown`-guarded), all fifteen leak.

**Tier 4 — direct `*Server` embeds (19 endpoints + 13 adapters).** Lowest risk: they name the type, so a signature change breaks the build at `agent/consul/server.go:1176`.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** every holder in Tier 1 and 2 must be proven to be *re-created* (not re-pointed) on server replacement; `Shutdown()` at `agent/consul/server.go:1241` must be shown to release each; `s.fsm.State().Abandon()` at `:1301` must fire before any holder in the "near-miss" FSM group is dropped; the double `rate.Handler` registration must be de-duplicated.

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** HashiCorp Consul — a service-mesh / service-discovery control plane. Go backend + an Ember UI (`ui/packages`, ignore for this ticket). Index: 3,297 files, 26,653 symbols; heaviest areas `agent/consul` (5,191 symbols), `agent/structs`, `agent`.

**Layout that matters here:**

| Path | Role |
|---|---|
| `agent/agent.go:234` | `Agent` — the process-level object; runs HTTP/DNS/gRPC and holds a `delegate` (`agent/agent.go:249`) that is *either* `*consul.Server` or `*consul.Client` |
| `agent/consul/server.go:169` | **`consul.Server` — the object being reworked.** ~275 lines of struct fields |
| `agent/consul/*_endpoint.go` | net/rpc endpoints, each embedding `*Server` |
| `agent/consul/*_backend.go`, `raft_handle.go`, `tenancy_bridge.go`, `v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go`, `acl_server.go`, `leader_connect_ca.go` | **adapter/wrapper types** — thin structs holding `*Server`, handed to subsystems as narrow interfaces |
| `agent/consul/server_grpc.go` | central gRPC wiring: `registerACLServer`, `registerPeerStreamServer`, … (lines 339–545) |
| `agent/grpc-external/services/*` | external gRPC services, each declaring its *own* local `Backend`/`ACLResolver`/`StateStore`/`FSMServer` interfaces |
| `agent/grpc-internal/services/subscribe` , `agent/rpc/{operator,peering}` | internal gRPC services, same pattern |
| `internal/storage/raft`, `internal/multicluster/...` | v2 resource stack, reaches the server through `Handle` / `AggregatedConfig` |

**The pattern to know:** almost nothing outside `agent/consul` names `*consul.Server`. Subsystems declare a local one-to-four-method interface and retain that. `agent/consul` supplies a satisfier — usually a one-field wrapper (`raftHandle{s *Server}`, `PeeringBackend{srv *Server}`) or `s` itself.

## Step 2 — The server lifecycle contract today

**Construction:** `consul.NewServer(config, flat Deps, externalGRPCServer, incomingRPCLimiter, serverLogger)` — `agent/consul/server.go:467`. Called from `agent.Agent.Start` (`agent/agent.go:600`, assigned at `agent/agent.go:709`). It is a single ~410-line constructor that builds the struct literal (`server.go:502–520`) and then wires every subsystem in place — there is no second `Start()` phase, so *construction is the wiring point*.

**Shutdown:** `(*Server).Shutdown()` — `agent/consul/server.go:1241`. Idempotent via `s.shutdown`; closes `s.shutdownCh` (`:1250`), stops leader routines (`:1254`), serf LAN/WAN, router, raft, listener, internal gRPC handler, conn pool, ACLResolver, then `s.fsm.State().Abandon()` (`:1301`). `(*Server).Leave()` — `agent/consul/server.go:1330` — graceful pre-shutdown. Reached from the agent at `agent/agent.go:1789` / `agent/agent.go:1707`.

**Long-lived state the server owns (outlives any request):**

| Field | file:line | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `fsm *fsm.FSM` | `agent/consul/server.go:237` | state store; `Abandon()`ed on shutdown |
| `raft *raft.Raft` + `raftStore` | `agent/consul/server.go:246`, `:158` | |
| `publisher *stream.EventPublisher` | `agent/consul/server.go:418` | run in a goroutine at `:570`, tied to `shutdownCh` |
| `serfLAN` / `serfWAN` | `agent/consul/server.go:348`, `:352` | |
| `router *router.Router` | `agent/consul/server.go:308` | |
| `connPool *pool.ConnPool` | `agent/consul/server.go:216` | |
| `shutdownCh` / `leaveCh` | `agent/consul/server.go:399`, `:275` | **the only cancellation signal** handed to ~12 background goroutines |
| `externalGRPCServer`, `internalGRPCHandler`, `rpcServer`, `insecureRPCServer` | `:277`, `:319`, `:320`, `:330` | |

**Layers between an incoming RPC and that state:**
1. `agent/consul/rpc.go` — net/rpc listener, conn limiter, `s.rpcServer` dispatch.
2. `agent/consul/rate.Handler` — `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:164`, registered with the server at `agent/consul/server.go:525`.
3. Endpoint structs registered at `agent/consul/server.go:1176` (`s.rpcServer.Register(fn(s))`) — e.g. `consul.Catalog` (`agent/consul/catalog_endpoint.go:78`).
4. `blockingquery.Query(s.FSMServer, …)` / `s.blockingQuery` — reads `s.fsm.State()`.
5. For gRPC: `agent/consul/server_grpc.go` registrars → service `Server{Config}` → its narrowed `Backend`/`GetStore` → back to `*Server`.

## Step 3 — How server access reaches a subsystem (worked trace)

Canonical hop chain, peerstream:

1. `agent/consul/server.go:467` — `NewServer` builds `s`.
2. `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:232` — `s.peeringBackend = NewPeeringBackend(s)`.
3. `agent/consul/peering_backend.go:47` — `NewPeeringBackend(srv *Server) *PeeringBackend` stores it at `agent/consul/peering_backend.go:33` (`srv *Server`) — **narrowing point**; declared satisfier of `peering.Backend` and `peerstream.Backend` at `agent/consul/peering_backend.go:39–40`.
4. `agent/consul/server_grpc.go:386` — `peerstream.NewServer(peerstream.Config{Backend: s.peeringBackend, GetStore: func() peerstream.StateStore { return s.FSM().State() }, ACLResolver: s.ACLResolver, …})`.
5. `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/server.go:36` — `Config.Backend Backend` — **retention point** (embedded into `Server` at `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/server.go:30`).
6. Second hop: `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/subscription_manager.go:46` — `backend SubscriptionBackend`, built at `stream_resources.go:345`; and `agent/grpc-external/services/peerstream/subscription_view.go:27` — `sub Subscriber`, forwarded into `submatview.LocalMaterializerDeps.Backend` at `subscription_view.go:78`.

Second canonical chain, ACL:
`agent/consul/server.go:633` `Backend: &serverACLResolverBackend{Server: s}` → `agent/consul/acl_server.go:110` (embeds `*Server`) → `agent/consul/acl.go:167` `ACLResolverConfig.Backend` → retained at `agent/consul/acl.go:281` `ACLResolver.backend`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius, ranked by risk

`sense_blast(consul.Server)`: **risk high**, 176 direct callers, 211 affected symbols, 80 affected files, 109 production / 61 test, 55 affected test files, 31 interface-retained holders.

**Tier 1 — no compile-time tie to `consul.Server`; a lifecycle change reaches them silently.** These are the ones that ship a dead handle.

1. **Closure-captured `s` in `server_grpc.go`** — `aclgrpc.Config` (5 closures, `server_grpc.go:357–370`), `GetStore` in peerstream/connectca/dataplane (`:387`, `:479`, `:499`), all `ForwardRPC` closures. Highest risk: a closure pins the *old* `*Server` with zero type signal anywhere, and `go vet`/compiler will never flag it. If the server is replaced these keep calling the dead one.
2. **`raft.Backend.handle` / `forwardingClient.handle`** — `internal/storage/raft/backend.go:80`, `forwarding.go:115`. Separate module tree, `Handle` interface, `b.Run(ctx)` goroutine started at `agent/consul/server.go:531` bound to `shutdownCh`. A dead handle here silently misroutes v2 resource writes.
3. **`v1compat.reconciler.config`** — `internal/multicluster/internal/controllers/v1compat/controller.go:82`; the shim also runs its own supervisor loop (`agent/consul/v2_config_entry_exports_shim.go:36`) with an `eventCh` that survives independently.
4. **`rate.Handler.serversStatusProvider`** — `agent/consul/rate/handler.go:168`, registered from *two* places (`server.go:525`, `agent/agent.go:708`). A stale provider makes `IsLeader()` lie to every RPC.
5. **`local.State.Delegate`** — `agent/local/state.go:172`; assigned once at `agent/agent.go:762` and never re-assigned. Anti-entropy would talk to a dead server forever.
6. **`agent.Agent.delegate`** — `agent/agent.go:249`; three concrete type assertions (`:1668`, `:1790`, `:1865`) that will compile fine but branch wrongly if the concrete type changes.
7. **`peerstream.subscriptionManager.backend` / `exportedServiceRequest.sub` / `submatview` materializer** — `subscription_manager.go:46`, `subscription_view.go:27,78`. Per-peering, long-lived streams; they outlive the registration call and hold the backend across reconnects.
8. **`ca.ConsulProvider.Delegate`** — `agent/connect/ca/provider_consul.go:35`, re-narrowed a second time at `leader_connect_ca.go:462`. Two indirections from the server; leaf signing goes wrong silently.

**Tier 2 — interface-typed but inside `agent/consul`, so a compile break is likely.** `ACLResolver.backend` (`acl.go:281`), `CAManager.delegate` (`leader_connect_ca.go:61`), `AutoConfig.backend` (`auto_config_endpoint.go:159`), `GatewayLocator.srv` (`gateway_locator.go:46`), `IndexReplicator.Delegate` (`replication.go:247`), `reporting.ReportingManager.server` (`reporting.go:17`).

**Tier 3 — background goroutines pinned to `shutdownCh`.** Every `go …(&lib.StopChannelContext{StopCh: s.shutdownCh})` at `agent/consul/server.go:531, 568, 570, 666, 670, 739, 752, 781, 784, 787, 788, 822, 832, 865, 1033`. If the rework introduces a new server without closing the old `shutdownCh` (`Shutdown` at `:1250` is the *only* closer, and it's `s.shutdown`-guarded), all fifteen leak.

**Tier 4 — direct `*Server` embeds (19 endpoints + 13 adapters).** Lowest risk: they name the type, so a signature change breaks the build at `agent/consul/server.go:1176`.

**Must be re-verified after the change:** every holder in Tier 1 and 2 must be proven to be *re-created* (not re-pointed) on server replacement; `Shutdown()` at `agent/consul/server.go:1241` must be shown to release each; `s.fsm.State().Abandon()` at `:1301` must fire before any holder in the "near-miss" FSM group is dropped; the double `rate.Handler` registration must be de-duplicated.
